title: Packera aurea
intro: Packera aurea, also known as golden ragwort or yellow top, is a perennial herb native to North America. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and soothe digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Packera aurea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Packera aurea is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Packera aurea is a species of flowering plant in the daisy and sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to eastern North America. Packera aurea is also known as golden ragwort or yellow ragwort, and it is a perennial herb. The plant is often found growing in woodland edges, rocky outcrops, and along streams. Packera aurea is commonly classified as a member of the genus Packera, which is a group of plants formerly classified as part of the genus Senecio.
